history.qwen_observation: UpgradePluginsForJava21 crashed with an IllegalAccessError inside OpenRewrite's internal Guava migration classes, indicating a classloader incompatibility in the runner's rewrite-maven-plugin version.
history.qwen_rationale: Removing the problematic UpgradePluginsForJava21 step avoids the internal classloader crash. UpgradeBuildToJava21 will handle the essential compiler and test plugin upgrades along with the JDK version bump, and java21_transforms will apply the remaining source-level migrations.

history.qwen_observation: IllegalAccessError in OpenRewrite's Guava migration recipe during UpgradeBuildToJava21 on JDK 21, caused by classloader isolation bugs in rewrite-maven-plugin 6.40.0 on JDK 21.
history.qwen_rationale: Running the build-upgrade recipes under JDK 17 avoids the JDK 21 classloader/module access restrictions that trigger the IllegalAccessError. The POM will be updated to target JDK 21, and subsequent source transformations will run under JDK 21 once the build configuration is in place.

history.qwen_observation: UpgradeBuildToJava21 triggered a classloader conflict with Guava migration recipes inside rewrite-maven-plugin:6.40.0, causing an IllegalAccessError during recipe execution.
history.qwen_rationale: Bypass the buggy UpgradeBuildToJava21 recipe by using a POM_PATCH step to force the maven-compiler-plugin to a JDK 21-compatible version (3.13.0), then run the Java 21 source transforms directly on JDK 21. This avoids loading the problematic Guava recipe classes while still achieving the code migration.

history.qwen_observation: OpenRewrite plugin v6.40.0 crashes with IllegalAccessError when loading rewrite-migrate-java recipes due to classloader isolation conflicts with Guava migration classes.
history.qwen_rationale: Bypass the broken recipe engine entirely by using a POM_PATCH step to force the maven-compiler-plugin to a JDK 21-compatible version and compile directly under JDK 21, avoiding the classloader conflict while achieving the target JDK migration.
